summary: Jeff Atwood discusses his search for a new mouse to address hand discomfort, settling on the Microsoft Habu as the ideal combination of classic Intellimouse ergonomics and Razer gaming technology. He explains the key features he values in mice: wired connection, high resolution sensor, thumb buttons, and adjustable DPI. The post then dives into the technical details of USB polling rates, explaining how higher polling rates reduce cursor latency. Atwood recommends increasing polling rate from the default 125 Hz to at least 500 Hz for noticeably smoother cursor movement, while acknowledging this level of optimization is just scratching the surface of what serious gamers pursue.
key_points: The Microsoft Habu combines the ergonomic body of the classic Intellimouse Explorer with Razer's gaming mouse technology
The Habu uniquely stores all settings in onboard flash memory, making it the first mouse Atwood owned with firmware that persists across computers
Default USB polling rate of 125 Hz limits cursor updates to every 8 milliseconds, but this can be increased via software or hardware to 1000 Hz (1 ms)
Higher-end mice like the Habu and recent Logitech models automatically increase USB polling rate in hardware when plugged in
The difference between 125 Hz and 500 Hz polling rate is perceptible to users sensitive to cursor smoothness
money_quotes: The Habu seems to have inherited the best traits from its parents: the classic body of the Intellimouse Explorer, with the sophisticated brains of a Razer gaming mouse.
It's the first mouse I've ever owned with firmware.
Once you've configured the settings to taste, you can unplug the mouse, bring it to another computer, and those settings will be retained.
If you're sensitive to cursor smoothness at all, I can practically guarantee you will feel the difference between 125 Hz and 500 Hz.
If you think all this talk of high DPI mice and USB polling rates is obsessive, trust me, it's merely the tip of the iceberg.
key_insight: Programmers who spend all day with a mouse in hand should pay attention to both ergonomics and technical specifications like DPI and USB polling rate, as these details meaningfully affect daily comfort and precision.
